case.paper_title: "A" Business by Any Other Name: Firm Name Choice as a Signal of Firm Quality
case.focal_claim: The average plumbing firm whose name begins with A or a number receives more service complaints than other firms.

original_datasets.name: final_data

summary_statistics.info: Rows: 2293; Columns: 19. Types: firm_id (object), others numeric (float32). Key analysis variables are complaints_2008 and first_A.

original_datasets.notes: Dataset appears to be a cleaned, aggregated file matching counts in the paper appendix for Illinois plumbing firms. Outcome uses overall complaints (complaints_2008). The indicator first_A denotes firm names beginning with 'A' or a number.

tasks.task_instruction: Conduct the analysis without additional restriction.

analysis_path.path_description: Compute mean overall complaints (complaints_2008) by first_A group and report difference/ratio.
preprocessing_steps: Treat first_A as a nominal/binary indicator.
Drop missing values for the selected variables.

key_choices.main_predictor_measure: Name begins with 'A' or a number (first_A).
key_choices.missing_data_rule: Drop rows with missing complaints_2008 or first_A.
key_choices.data_processing: Group by first_A and compute means, standard deviations, and counts.

outcome.name: complaints_2008
outcome.definition: Total number of BBB complaints recorded in 2008 for the firm.

main_predictor.name: first_A
main_predictor.definition: Indicator that firm name begins with 'A' or a number (1=yes, 0=no).

analysis_code.notes: Computes group means and ratio for complaints_2008 by first_A. Reads /app/data/final_data.dta and writes /app/data/task1_output.json.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Follow this planned path; do not change the focal claim, dataset, or analysis goal.

tasks.task_instruction: Use only the data of Illinois plumbing firms; do not restrict to metro Chicago; do not include Yelp, Angie's List, or Consumer's Checkbook reviews; use overall complaints (not per employee).

analysis_path.path_description: Run a two-sample t-test (Welch) comparing overall complaints between first_A groups.
preprocessing_steps: Treat first_A as a nominal/binary indicator.
Exclude review-related variables and do not restrict to Chicago.
Drop missing values for the selected variables.

key_choices.main_predictor_measure: Name begins with 'A' or a number (first_A).
key_choices.sample_restriction: None beyond using the Illinois plumbing firm dataset.
key_choices.missing_data_rule: Drop rows with missing complaints_2008 or first_A.
key_choices.data_processing: Convert first_A to binary indicator and split into two groups.

outcome.name: complaints_2008
outcome.definition: Total number of BBB complaints recorded in 2008 for the firm.

main_predictor.name: first_A
main_predictor.definition: Indicator that firm name begins with 'A' or a number (1=yes, 0=no).

analysis_code.notes: Implements the JMP Oneway t-test in Python using Welch's t-test. Reads /app/data/final_data.dta and writes /app/data/task2_output.json.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Use the planned t-test path; do not add controls or alternate outcomes; do not restrict the sample to Chicago.

files.data/task1_DAA9C__py.py: Python script to compute group means and ratio of complaints_2008 by first_A for Task1.
files.data/task2_DAA9C__py.py: Python script to run a Welch two-sample t-test of complaints_2008 by first_A for Task2.

codebase.notes: Original reanalysis artifacts include a Jupyter Notebook (Multi100_analysis_DAA9C.ipynb) and a JMP script (Multi100_analysis_DAA9C_step2.jsl). Per run policy, these have been translated to Python scripts that read from /app/data/final_data.dta.

analysis.instructions: Execute Task1 and Task2 using the original dataset final_data.dta and produce structured outputs in /app/data. Do not modify the dataset or analysis paths.
not_allowed_actions: replace the planned path with a different analysis path

debugging_rule.repair_scope: fix dependency, file path, or output extraction issues only; do not redesign the analysis.

analysis.comparison_metrics: Task1: group means, difference in means, ratio of means. Task2: t-statistic and two-sided p-value from Welch's t-test.
